message:
fix(sync): replace timestamp/content-hash comparisons with sync_equals() in 3-way merge

The 3-way merge logic (Cases 4-7) used updated_at timestamps and content_hash
comparisons to detect changes, which silently lost modifications to labels,
dependencies, and comments when the title+description content hash was unchanged.

- Cases 4-5: replaced `l.updated_at > b.updated_at` with `!l.sync_equals(b)`
  to detect all sync-relevant field changes, not just timestamp ordering
- Cases 6-7: replaced `l.content_hash == r.content_hash` with `l.sync_equals(r)`
  to compare full sync state instead of just content
- sync.rs: add merge resolution notes as comments on affected issues after
  3-way merge completes, providing visibility into conflict resolution decisions
- Add regression test (tests/repro_3way_merge_bug.rs) reproducing the data loss
  scenario where local label changes were lost during merge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
